title: The Royal "Spectator Sport" Menagerie (Pre-Revolution France)
script: Imagine giving birth... while two hundred strange men watch you from inches away. In pre-revolution France, queens had absolutely zero privacy in the delivery room! Because the royal bloodline had to be undeniably proven, the law required a massive crowd to witness a royal birth to ensure no 'fake' babies were smuggled in. When Marie Antoinette gave birth in 1778, her bedroom was instantly flooded by over two hundred courtiers, lords, and politicians. There were so many fancy aristocrats crammed around her bed, craning their necks to watch, that the room ran out of oxygen and she actually passed out from the heat! Would you survive a royal birth? Let us know in the comments!

scenes.prompt: Hyper-realistic portrait photography of a jaw-droppingly gorgeous 23-year-old French queen with flawless porcelain skin, powdered platinum hair in an ornate updo, wearing a pristine white silk regal nightgown, sitting up in a lavish gilded palace bed. She stares directly at the camera with a nervous, wide-eyed expression. Vibrant colors, hyper-saturated, 8k resolution, cinematic lighting, documentary style realism, highly detailed, shot on 35mm lens, photorealistic. No painting.
